title: The Senses – Young Children (Ages 6–8)

hunks.text: Your body has five amazing SENSES. Senses help you learn about the world around you. You have EYES, EARS, a NOSE, a MOUTH, and SKIN. Each sense does something special. They tell your brain what is happening near you.

questions.question: How many senses do you have?
Name a body part that helps you sense things.
Which sense do you use the most every day?
Close your eyes and think about your favorite food. What do you sense about it?

hunks.text: Your EYES help you SEE. You see colors, shapes, and light. Your eyes help you see a red apple, a blue sky, and your mom's face. Seeing helps you move around safely. It helps you find things you want to play with.

questions.question: What do your eyes help you do?
Name something you can see with your eyes.
questions.answer: COLORS / SHAPES / LIGHT / APPLE / SKY / FACE

questions.question: Imagine looking at a rainbow. What colors do you see?
questions.answer: RED / ORANGE / YELLOW / GREEN / BLUE / PURPLE

questions.question: What is the most beautiful thing you like to look at?

hunks.text: Your EARS help you HEAR. You hear SOUNDS all day long. You hear your friend's VOICE, a DOG barking, music, and birds singing. Your ears listen to LOUD sounds and QUIET sounds. Hearing helps you know what is happening around you.

questions.question: What do your ears help you do?
Name a sound you can hear.
questions.answer: VOICE / BARKING / MUSIC / SINGING / LOUD / QUIET

questions.question: Close your eyes and listen. What sounds do you hear right now?
What is your favorite sound to hear?

hunks.text: Your NOSE helps you SMELL. Every food and flower has its own SMELL. You can SMELL pizza cooking, fresh FLOWERS, and rain. Your nose tells your brain what SCENT you are near. A good smell makes you happy. A bad smell warns you to stay away.

questions.question: What do your nose help you do?
Name something that has a smell.
Imagine you are near a bakery. What smell do you sense?
What is a smell you really like?

hunks.text: Your MOUTH helps you TASTE. Your tongue is inside your mouth and it TASTES food. Some foods taste SWEET, like candy and fruit. Some foods taste SALTY, like chips. Some foods taste SOUR, like lemons. Your tongue helps you enjoy eating and keeps you safe from food that is bad.

questions.question: What do you use to taste food?
Name a taste you can feel in your mouth.
Imagine eating a lemon. What taste do you feel on your tongue?
What food tastes the best to you?

hunks.text: Your SKIN helps you TOUCH and FEEL. Your skin covers your whole body. When you TOUCH something, your skin tells your brain about it. Things can feel SOFT, HARD, WARM, COLD, or ROUGH. Your skin protects you and helps you know if something is safe to touch.

questions.question: What body part helps you touch things?
Name a way something can feel when you touch it.
Close your eyes and touch something. What does it feel like?
What texture do you like to touch the most?

hunks.text: All five SENSES work together to help you. When you eat, you SEE the food, SMELL it, TASTE it, and FEEL its texture. When you play outside, you HEAR birds, SEE trees, SMELL flowers, and FEEL the wind. Your SENSES help you enjoy life and stay safe.

questions.question: Name something your senses help you do.
questions.answer: ENJOY LIFE / STAY SAFE / LEARN / PLAY

questions.question: Imagine playing at the park. What do you see, hear, and feel?
questions.answer: TREES / SWINGS / BIRDS / WIND / GRASS / FRIENDS

questions.question: When do you use all your senses at the same time?
What is something your eyes help you do besides see?

hunks.text: Your SENSES are amazing gifts. Take time every day to notice what you SEE, HEAR, SMELL, TASTE, and FEEL. Your senses help you discover new things and stay connected to the world around you. Be grateful for your five wonderful senses!

questions.question: Which sense is most important to you?
Think about your favorite place. What does it feel like to be there with all your senses?
Name one sense you use every single day.
How do your senses help you have fun?

citations: National Institutes of Health (NIH) - The Five Senses: https://www.nih.gov/health-topics
American Academy of Pediatrics - Child Development and the Senses
Scholastic Inc. - The Five Senses Unit for Elementary Learners
